hero.description: RadwaMinta has been producing, processing, and exporting high-quality herbs, spices and seeds since 2006. With global branches and partnerships, we deliver authentic products and ensure excellence in every step, making us your trusted resource worldwide.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)
faq.subtitle: We've gathered all your common questions here, so you don't have to search

questions.q1: What is the source of your herbs?
questions.a1: All our herbs and botanicals are locally grown on our farms in Fayoum, Egypt.
questions.q2: Are your herbs dried industrially or naturally?
questions.a2: We rely on natural sun-drying methods at our facilities. This preserves the natural color and the full nutritional and therapeutic value of the herbs, unlike industrial drying which can diminish their properties.
questions.q3: Are your products available outside of the growing season?
questions.a3: Yes. Our herbs are guaranteed to be available year-round (Whole Year Availability). We achieve this through careful inventory management and professional storage practices, ensuring consistent supply even beyond the harvest season.
questions.q4: Are your products packaged in an eco-friendly way?
questions.a4: Yes, we prioritize sustainability. We rely on 3-ply paper bags and carton boxes as outer packaging for most products. All our packaging materials are untreated, eco-friendly, and ensure that our herbs remain safe, natural, and well-preserved. For ground and cut herbs, we use the same sustainable paper-based packaging to maintain product quality while protecting the environment.
questions.q5: What is the best time to purchase our products?
questions.a5: The ideal time to source our products is during the harvest season, ensuring maximum freshness, quality, and cost efficiency.

contact.title: Specifications to Meet Your Needs
contact.description: Need a quote or technical support? Fill out the form below, and our dedicated team will respond within hours.

story.description: We are more than a company; we are a movement to reconnect the world with the treasures of Egyptian nature...

reviews.subtitle: Real Results. Authentic Reviews. Discover the Difference Nature Makes

hero.subtitle1: The raw material passes through several steps
hero.description: From Queen Hatshepsut's incense trees to modern herbal science, our story bridges history and today.

hero_legacy.text2: (1479–1458 BC), who established Egypt's aromatic trade with incense and myrrh.
hero_legacy.text3: today, cultivating the same ancient treasures—basil, chamomile, mint, and black seed—to enrich lives globally.

roots.title: A Legacy That Blossomed Thousands of Years Ago

founder.title: The Heir of Hatshepsut
founder.subtitle: Dr. Radwa was born with the spirit of ancient Egypt's gardens.
founder.description:
She combines modern science with sustainable farming to bring Egypt's fields back to life.
She is the powerful vision crafting pure products—the modern echo of Hatshepsut's story.

vision.description: Egyptian excellence that will make the world healthier.

message.description: We encourage the entire world to revert to sustainable natural products by providing the best high-quality products at competitive prices, always working in a safe and clean environment to win the trust of all customers.

farms.title: From Egypt to the World
farms.paragraph_1: RadwaMinta has vast areas of farms where we pay great attention to the quality of cultivation, watering, and harvesting. Our farms are constantly updated to comply with the latest local and international standards. We are regularly inspected to ensure the highest purity standards. We carry out regular analysis tests on our farm products to ensure minimal bacterial count without any pathogenic microbes. In addition, the company follows hygiene, sanitation procedures, and a quality assurance system.
farms.paragraph_2: Our farms are distinguished by their vast areas, being far from polluted regions to provide the ideal atmosphere for our plants.
farms.paragraph_3: Our farms are also famous for their rich, productive, and pure soil capable of producing the finest herbs.
farms.pre_harvest_text: This stage includes the growing process, which is carried out without chemicals, irradiation, or ozone treatment. Crops are grown, harvested, and processed without pesticides, chemical fertilizers, or herbicides. Instead, natural materials are used to provide plants with essential nutrients. This stage also includes the daily monitoring of plants.
farms.post_harvest_text: This stage includes the drying process, packing, and enveloping.
farms.drying_process_text: It is carried out in a roofed area under indirect sunlight. Each product has its own code along with the farm code. Then, a sample is sent to the central lab for microbiological testing.
farms.paragraph_4: Another sample is sent to the Agricultural Research Center to analyze the product for the percentage of essential oils it contains.
farms.qcap_text: Central Laboratory for Residue Analysis of Pesticides and Heavy Metals in Food.

quality.title: Quality
quality.subtitle: When it comes to wellness, every detail matters.
quality.first_paragraph: At RadwaMinta, there is no such thing as an “off-season” item. We know how to store every product under the best and most suitable conditions to maintain its quality. However, we always prefer to provide each product during its natural season to ensure maximum freshness and authenticity.
quality.second_paragraph: Our quality and safety measures strictly comply with international standards. We implement precise, up-to-date, and cutting-edge practices to guarantee the highest level of protection for our customers’ health.
quality.third_paragraph: Beyond that, our Quality Control Department is continuously seeking new ideas and innovative approaches to enhance and safeguard product quality. Our ultimate goal is to protect not only our customers, but also the environment, the soil, the water, and the community around us.
quality.quality_control_description: Quality Control Sample Distribution and Testing
quality.raw_material_sample_p1: From every batch of raw materials, samples are collected from different depths of each sack and then combined into one composite. This composite is divided into ten sub-samples, each labeled with the necessary details such as farm name, crop, plot location, date, and protocol.
quality.raw_material_sample_p2: The sub-samples are tested for visual quality, pesticide residues, microbial and aflatoxin levels, essential oils, and moisture content. To ensure traceability, extra reference samples are securely stored for at least two years, in line with the product’s shelf life.
quality.raw_material_sample_p3: All results are carefully recorded and archived in the company’s quality system to guarantee safety, consistency, and transparency.
quality.final_product_sample_p1: Final product sampling follows the same rigorous protocols established for raw materials. Each batch is carefully inspected and tested to confirm consistency, purity, and compliance with the defined standards.
quality.final_product_sample_p2: In addition, an advanced metal detection analysis is performed as an extra safety measure, eliminating any possible risk of contamination. All results are recorded, documented, and archived within the company’s quality system to guarantee traceability and full accountability.
quality.packing_description: Standardized Packaging Material Specifications and Labeling Procedures for Traceability and Compliance
quality.packing_p1: the products are packed in three-layer paper bags, with the outer seal secured mechanically.
quality.packing_p2: the same three-layer paper bags are used with a mechanical outer seal, in addition to an inner plastic liner sealed with a releasable cable tie to ensure maximum protection.
quality.packing_p3: the products are packed in carton boxes to preserve their integrity and quality.
quality.packing_p4: All packages are clearly labeled with our company label and sealed according to international standards. Packing materials are completely untreated, and every product carries the statement “100% Clean.” Each label includes essential details such as weight, batch/lot number, production date, origin, and expiry date to guarantee traceability and compliance.

Quality Management Qualification for Medicinal and Aromatic Plants – Shell Corporation
International Export Logistics and Shipping – Fayoum University
Global Quality Systems Training – ISO 22000, HACCP, ISO 9001, Organic (EEC & NOP)

experience.overviewText: Radwa provides consulting services for emerging companies in the export of medicinal and aromatic plants — helping them build strong foundations to compete effectively in global markets.

businessManagementPoints.1: Preparing companies to enter and operate effectively in the export market
businessManagementPoints.2: Developing management systems and workflow efficiency
businessManagementPoints.3: Supervising all departments and ensuring quality control procedures

marketingPoints.1: Building marketing strategies for international buyers
marketingPoints.2: Training teams on negotiation and communication skills
marketingPoints.3: Enhancing the company’s image and brand positioning in global markets

exportOpsPoints.1: Guidance on export papers, permits, and compliance requirements
exportOpsPoints.2: Advising on shipping methods, delivery terms (Incoterms), and logistics planning
exportOpsPoints.3: Drafting payment terms and supporting banking transactions with importers

pricingPoints.1: Setting competitive pricing strategies for global markets
pricingPoints.2: Supervising product preparation and packaging according to international standards

certificationsPoints.1: Assisting companies in obtaining international certifications
certificationsPoints.2: Helping them meet qualification requirements to gain a competitive advantage

community.intro_title: The change starts in the field, not in the office.
community.intro_paragraph: Since 2006, Radwa has been moving across Egypt, turning her story in exporting medicinal and aromatic plants into a source of inspiration for farmers, students, and young entrepreneurs.
community.fayoum_workshop: Fayoum. A workshop funded by GEMISA brought together rural women looking for hope in sustainable farming.
community.radwa_journey: Radwa stood before them — not as a lecturer, but as someone who had walked the same path — sharing her own journey and proving that passion and persistence can turn agriculture into a life-changing story.
community.south_valley_univ: At South Valley University, in collaboration with UNIDO's Green Trade Initiative, Radwa introduced an idea that would reshape how youth see agriculture: learn it by living it.
community.initiative_impact: Her 'Little Exporter Initiative' opened the door for students to work directly with farms and exporters, transforming theory into action.
community.year_of_youth: The Year of Youth and Change
community.radwa_stage: From 'It's Time' to 'The Successful Agricultural Engineer,' Radwa took the stage in universities across Egypt.
community.students_build: She challenged students to stop waiting for opportunities — to build them.
community.employment_forum: In Fayoum University's Employment Forum, she showed them real footage from the fields, letting them see what export looks like beyond the classroom.
community.seminars_conferences: Between seminars and conferences, Radwa worked with researchers at Fayoum University to connect scientific studies with market realities.
community.research_meets_soil: She believed that progress happens when research meets soil — when the lab talks to the land.
community.voice_farmers_earth: A Voice for Farmers and the Earth
community.radwa_office: To this day, Radwa keeps her office open to students, researchers, and dreamers.
community.questions_to_action: She helps them turn their questions into action, and their projects into impact.
community.success_shared: Because for her, success only matters when it's shared.

Initiative.little_exporter_subtitle: Building a new generation of young Egyptian exporters
Initiative.idea_title: The Idea – Where It All Began
Initiative.idea_paragraph_1_part1: Born from the mind of a young Egyptian woman passionate about her field, the
Initiative.idea_paragraph_1_part2: was launched by Radwa ElKalifa, the youngest professional working in Egypt’s medicinal and aromatic plant export sector.
Initiative.idea_paragraph_2_part2: Radwa launched the initiative for the first time
Initiative.idea_paragraph_3_part1: Her vision was clear and inspiring:
Initiative.idea_paragraph_3_bold1: “An Egyptian product made by the hands of Egyptian youth.”
Initiative.idea_paragraph_3_part2: A movement that connects universities, farmers, and companies to build a new generation ready to export proudly.
Initiative.dream_title: From a Personal Dream to a National Effort
Initiative.dream_paragraph_1: Radwa invited agricultural faculties, associations, and major export companies to join hands and grow the initiative.
Initiative.dream_paragraph_2: It gained national attention, appearing on TV and radio, and Radwa was invited by UNIDO to present it at the Youth Employment Forum in South Valley University, Luxor — where it received praise from students, professors, and government officials alike.
Initiative.motivation_title: The Motivation – Why She Did It
Initiative.motivation_1: 1. Her strong belief in developing Egypt’s export sector for medicinal and aromatic plants.
Initiative.motivation_2: 2. Her desire to solve challenges at every stage of the export process — from harvesting and processing to approval by importers.
Initiative.motivation_3: 3. Her aim to train agriculture graduates and enthusiasts through practical, hands-on learning in Fayoum’s rich plant ecosystem.
Initiative.motivation_4: 4. Her effort to boost Egypt’s exports and national income by bringing foreign currency and supporting the economy during hard times.
Initiative.motivation_5_part1: 5. Her ambition to create a globally competitive Egyptian product with a local identity —
Initiative.motivation_5_bold1: “An Egyptian product made by the hands of Egyptian youth.”
Initiative.content_title: The Content – Three Days of Learning and Action
Initiative.content_day1_part2: Theoretical sessions about export fundamentals, company structure, and legal procedures for establishing an export business.
Initiative.content_day2_part2: Practical fieldwork — visiting farms, observing harvesting, drying, processing, and packaging stages.
Initiative.content_day3_part2: Final sessions on loading containers, export logistics, and real problem-solving discussions with participants.
Initiative.impact_paragraph_1: The Little Exporter Initiative stands as a remarkable milestone in Radwa’s mission to empower her community and support Egypt’s herbal export industry.
Initiative.impact_paragraph_2: It’s more than an initiative — it’s a movement of empowerment and knowledge.
Initiative.impact_paragraph_3: And the journey continues… through digital programs, partnerships, and a growing network of young Egyptians who believe they can reach the world.

section1.title: Beginnings
section1.subtitle: The Spark of a Dream
section1.text: Radwa ElKhalifa’s journey began with a dream that many thought was impossible — to make her mark in the male-dominated world of exporting medicinal and aromatic plants. Coming from Fayoum, she carried not privilege but persistence. With nothing but personal savings, ambition, and belief in herself, she started from the bottom and steadily carved her way to the top.

section2.title: Resilience
section2.subtitle: Turning Challenges into Strength
section2.text: Her path was never easy. Facing skepticism and barriers as a woman in business, Radwa transformed every challenge into motivation. She worked tirelessly to prove that expertise and determination know no gender. Over the years, she built “RadwaMinta,” a brand that stands for quality, honesty, and Egyptian excellence.

section3.title: Gratitude & Legacy
section3.subtitle: The Power Behind Her Success
section3.text: Behind Radwa’s achievements stands a story of gratitude — to her parents for their values, her children for their inspiration, and her country for its spirit. Her philosophy is simple yet powerful: success isn’t given; it’s earned. Today, she dedicates her journey to every Egyptian woman who refuses to give up on her dream.

media.title: Media Presence
media.subtitle: Moments That Tell Her Story
media.text: Through these interviews, Radwa shares her story, her dreams, and her belief in the power of persistence.

certificates.title: RadwaMint’s Certificates
certificates.food_safety_desc: RadwaMinta is certified as the first center for packaging medicinal and aromatic plants in Fayoum and Egypt, led by a businesswoman on the White List of the Egyptian Food Safety Authority.
certificates.iso_desc: RadwaMinta is ISO 22000 certified, ensuring that all processes meet international standards for food safety and quality management, from sourcing to final packaging.

about_us.title: About us
about_us.subtitle: Natural products for a sustainable healthy life
about_us.paragraph_1_rest: is an Egyptian company working in the field of herbs since 2006 for producing, processing, and exporting herbs, spices, seeds, as well as natural oils of herbs and seeds. In 2011, we became the representative office for Millennium Herbs, a company in the USA that has a branch in Italy and another in China. So we are a global company with four branches around the world.
about_us.paragraph_2_part2: of business and work ethics to reach our goals, summarized as follows: to be your global resource for any of our A1 products and to gain the confidence of our clients.
about_us.paragraph_3_part1: So we guarantee that you will have
about_us.paragraph_3_part2: with top-notch quality through continuous documentation and comprehensive testing – from growing and harvesting, right through processing and up to the finished product.
about_us.paragraph_4_part3: all over the world as a result of being actively cooperating with local and

about_us.paragraph_5_part2: ever as these products are ours, they basically come from our farms.
